statement.en: ESGE recommends the best practice technique described below for retrieval of polyp tissue after EMR.

text.en: Change patient position to respect the influence of gravity on fluid/blood (away from the lesion) and to optimize views.
Excellent basic colonoscopy technique to arrive at the lesion without looping.
Use of external pressure to prevent loop formation during insertion.
Use of different endoscopes with different lengths, stiffness, and tip-bending properties etc. (e. g. pediatric colonoscope, gastroscope, balloon-enteroscope).
Ask assistant/nurse to hold the endoscope as needed.
Use retroflexion where possible and appropriate.
Use of a transparent distal attachment (e. g. for flexures, ileocecal valve, or dentate line).
Resection technique: commence resection in a way that facilitates access to the most difficult part of the lesion early in the procedure to optimize the chance of complete resection.
Resection technique: use submucosal lifting to promote access to the required part of the lesion.
Resection technique: use snare resection of the surrounding lifting mucosa to create a step for snare placement on difficult-to-capture tissue.
Resection technique: create a step into the submucosal plane to assist snare placement (hybrid-EMR).
Resection technique: use an avulsion technique such as cold avulsion.
Consider asking for a second opinion from a more experienced colleague at the time or before a second attempt. Consider that you may not be the best person to perform the second attempt.
Try a second attempt at EMR prior to surgery (two-stage EMR).
